response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates strong general software engineering expertise with 15+ years of experience across multiple domains, this role has critical disqualifications. Most critically, native-level Japanese proficiency is a required qualification and is completely absent from the resume. Additionally, the technical domain expertise—embedded networking security, kernel-level Linux work, security technologies (MACsec, 802.1X), and networking protocols (NETCONF, SNMP)—is not evident in their background. The candidate's C programming experience dates back to 2014-2015 and has been replaced by primarily web-stack work (TypeScript/JavaScript). The combination of the mandatory language requirement and lack of core technical domain knowledge makes this a poor fit for the role.
strengths: Extensive 15+ years of software development experience across web, mobile, and embedded systems
Strong background in development infrastructure, CI/CD, Docker, and modern software craftsmanship practices (TDD, pairing)
Some embedded systems exposure through robotic arm development and LabWindows/CVI work with C/C++
concerns: Native-level Japanese language proficiency is a required qualification and is completely absent—candidate lists only English (fluent), Romanian, and basic German
No demonstrated expertise in security technologies (MACsec, IEEE 802.1X, encryption, attack detection) or embedded networking (Layer-2/3, NETCONF, SNMP, Telemetry)
C programming proficiency appears outdated (last professional C work in 2014-2015); recent 10+ years focused on TypeScript/JavaScript web development rather than systems/embedded work

key_factors.factor: Current C and Linux kernel proficiency
